reasoning: FIDE ratings are updated on the 1st of each month. Therefore, on August 24, Harika Dronavalli's FIDE ranking will unquestionably be exactly what it was on August 7 (Rank #12). This makes resolution for the first date an almost certain YES (rank <= 12). For future months, we must anticipate natural rating fluctuations. As of the July/August rating lists, Dronavalli (#12) sits on a very slim margin above Mariya Muzychuk (#13), with only a 2-point rating difference (2488 vs 2486). Furthermore, younger, rapidly improving Indian prospects like 19-year-old Divya Deshmukh (who just won the 2025 Women's World Cup and jumped to #15) are actively gaining rating points and placing heavy downward pressure on the rankings of veteran players. Because of this, it is slightly more likely that Dronavalli will drift down to #13 or #14 than successfully push upward, as she needs to overcome a larger 14-point gap to overtake #11 Nana Dzagnidze. As the forecasting horizon extends into September, November, and next February, the probability of her effectively defending her #12 position slowly drops due to rating variance and the strength of rising juniors, anchoring towards 0.5 to accurately represent the increased uncertainty over time.
